reviews.review_msg: Crim 432 online with her was the worst class ever. We had two thirty page papers one for the first 6 weeks and one for the last 6 weeks. It was horrible. The exams were 5 essay questions and the questions were obscure, not at all the repeated themes in the course. Would not recommend unless you want to waste your time.

Joan Brockman (professor) and Sarah Yercich (TM) are a powerful team! Great class, amazing support, all around positive experience. Would recommend as long as Sarah is your TM... this class would have been a lot more difficult without her support and guidance.

CRIM 437 + 436. VERY good at making us critical thinkers and receptive to student feedback.  I was intimated by her and she seems cold at first but she actually cares a lot about her students and is a fair marker. First seminars I ever took and I'm glad it was with her. Presentation + 4800 papers were killer but no exams for her seminars.

Joan is a very knowledgeable professor and has a lot of research experience. Her class was informative, but the slideshows she uses are repetitive and shows too many pointless videos. There is a 5000-5500 word paper that is killer to write. This professor did not feel apparoachable, and she always rushed out of class as soon as it was over.

Not as tough as I expected. Two exams (25% each) verbatim from the study questions. Two 750 word papers (10% each), two 1-hour BBC discussions (10% each), and a short research assignment (10%). As long as you read the textbook and do all the study questions, you will be fine. You get to create your own topic for the two short papers.

Good class. Very interesting. Great material.   Brockman is great (!!!)but since its distance ed you don't speak with her much.  There is a lot of reading and writing, but its interesting and easy if you stay on top of it. The class would have been more tough if the TA wasn't so good. The TA could make or break this class, and Sarah was great.

Expect to have a lot of reading for this class. There are two 8,000+ assignments due, along with a mid-term and the final. The material is very dry and repetitive and if you want a good grade prepare to write a paper every week. If you need to take a crim course for credits, I suggest you take a different one.

Get ready to WRITE! Joan is awesome and thought-provoking. Course structure is AWESOME, students pretty much taught themselves with Joan facilitating (discussions + last 3 weeks were just presentations). Learned a LOT. DO NOT SKIP CLASS, participating is big! Do all your reading and assignment on time and you will get an easy A. Great class!

This is by far one of my favorite classes that I have taken at SFU. However, you do need to keep up with the readings in order to do well. Instructions are clear and concepts are straightforward. MT/Final consisted of short answers and were fair. TA Kouri was very helpful and provided great feedback. Highly recommended!

The class average for the midterm is very poor, and apparently that is common. She does not offer a "no double jeopardy" rule, so you can be penalized for answering one question wrong on all subsequent questions that rely on it. The material is not that bad but the test format is terrible.
